title: Steps to Category Creation
author: Aberdeen Group
abstract: Working draft of Aberdeen Group's 10-step category creation methodology presentation, developed for the Maxtor midline storage engagement circa 2003. Enumerates and briefly describes each step: Define, Differentiate, Debunk, Define argument/position/messages, Deliver to influencers, Create awareness, Evangelize, Measure and report, Let influencers take over, and Nurture. Includes several placeholder slides indicating this was a working/incomplete version.
importance_rationale: Documents Aberdeen Group's proprietary methodology for engineering a new IT market category; represents the intellectual property core of their Maxtor engagement and provides rare insight into how analyst firms operationalize category creation.
relevance_rationale: 10-step framework is directly applicable to any technology category launch; Aberdeen's distinction between analyst/press/Wall Street delivery paths and the CRM precedent of letting influencers adopt and rename the concept remains best practice in category marketing.
prescience_rationale: Framework correctly predicts that measurement and independent thought leadership are required for category success; CRM precedent cited (Gartner adoption) proved accurate model; midline as a concept did achieve partial category status before Maxtor's 2006 acquisition.
